---
title: ""
output: 
  html_document:
    self contained: true
date: ""
---

```{r setup, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(plotly)
il_counties_pct <- readRDS("~/Desktop/final-project-Smlfberry/data/il_counties_pct.rds")
il_edu <- il_counties_pct %>%
  mutate(pct_college = pct_bachelors + pct_grad)
```

```{r make groups, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

il_edu <- il_edu |>
  mutate(edu_quartile = ntile(pct_college, 4),
         edu_group = case_when(
           edu_quartile == 1 ~ "Under 18%",
           edu_quartile == 2 ~ "18–21%",
           edu_quartile == 3 ~ "21–26%",
           edu_quartile == 4 ~ "Over 26%"
         ),
         edu_group = factor(edu_group, levels = c("Under 18%", "18–21%", "21–26%", "Over 26%")))

```

```{r bar chart, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=6, fig.height=4.5, out.width="100%"}
il_edu_avg <- il_edu |>
  group_by(edu_group) |>
  summarise(avg_turnout = mean(turnout_rate, na.rm = TRUE))
p <- ggplot(il_edu_avg, aes(x = edu_group, y = avg_turnout, fill = avg_turnout)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#c8e0f0", high = "#1a3a5c") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0.55, 0.70)) +
  labs(x = "Share of residents with a college degree", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12, family = "Georgia"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 11, family = "Georgia"),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#faf9f6", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#faf9f6", color = NA)
  )
p
```
